title: Artifact Footprint By Class
goal: Implement #438 by adding a compact artifact-footprint-by-class summary to report output without changing ordinary startup.
non_goals: Do not turn footprint reporting into a dashboard.
Do not make historical archives, reviews, or generated outputs ordinary startup input.

intent.outcome: agentic-workspace report --section operational_compression exposes compact artifact classes, counts, pressure, and one cleanup/review target when present.
intent.latitude: Implement the smallest file-counting/report helper and regression tests.
intent.escalation: Escalate if accurate footprint reporting requires broad deletion policy or a new top-level command.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit the completed slice, close #438, then continue the next footprint-pressure lane.
execution.proof: workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning surfaces, report/proof dogfooding

invariants: Footprint output is compact and selector-driven.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #436/#449 make artifact growth visible and keep package use cheaper than bypassing it.

required_continuation.activation trigger: after #438, continue #439-#441 retention/generated-output slices unless the compact report proves them already satisfied

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add compact operational footprint reporting by artifact class.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not expand startup or make cleanup destructive.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact class names, thresholds, and test shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The slice needs retention/deletion policy beyond reporting.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Make existing report selector carry the footprint answer instead of adding a command.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can an agent see artifact pressure by class without reading archives, reviews, Memory, docs, or generated outputs?

immediate_next_action: Archive, commit, push, and close #438 with proof; then refresh and select the next open footprint/compression lane.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section operational_compression --format json

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/artifact-footprint-by-class.plan.json src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Classes distinguish live, historical, durable, generated, local-only, and large docs/package surfaces.
Output names at least one cleanup or review target when pressure exists.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: operational_compression now includes artifact_footprint_by_class with compact counts, roles, pressure, and one recommended cleanup/review target.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: pytest workspace CLI, ruff, planning surface check, operational_compression report, proof selection, root CLI authority, planning doctor, start, and default report all passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: #439 archive retention, #440 review retention, #441 generated output footprint, under #436/#449.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: archived plan and issue closeout proof
execution_summary.resume from: external issue refresh, then next open footprint/compression lane

discard.summary: individual validation command transcripts after proof and commit

continuation.summary: #439 should use artifact_footprint_by_class.archived_execplans as one input, not reread all archives by default.
#440 should use artifact_footprint_by_class.review_artifacts as one input, not make review artifacts ordinary startup state.
#441 should use artifact_footprint_by_class.generated_outputs as one input and keep generated artifacts derived.

config_check.summary: tests/test_workspace_cli.py guards artifact_footprint_by_class as selector-driven operational_compression output.

issue_follow_up.summary: Keep #436 and #449 open as parent lanes.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Add compact operational footprint reporting by artifact class.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: operational_compression now includes artifact_footprint_by_class; tests cover class roles and pressure recommendations; startup and default report remain compact routers.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane operational-footprint-pressure activates a fresh bounded slice.

cost_assessment.workflow_cost_found: Before this slice, an agent had to infer artifact pressure by reading multiple historical, memory, generated, and local surfaces.
cost_assessment.architecture_cost_found: Long-running artifacts existed across several directories with no compact class-level report.
cost_assessment.needless_complexity_found: A new command would have added surface area; the final change uses the existing operational_compression selector.
cost_assessment.surfaces_added: No new first-contact or top-level command; one compact selector payload was added.
cost_assessment.surfaces_removed_merged_or_demoted: Footprint inspection is backgrounded behind --section operational_compression.
cost_assessment.artifact_footprint_changed: One active plan will archive after closeout; existing artifact classes are now visible in a compact report.
cost_assessment.shipped_default_footprint_changed: Ordinary startup and default report remain router-shaped.
signals_consumed: dogfooding need to see artifact growth without manual directory scans
cost_assessment.human_steering_avoided_next_time: A future agent can query one selector for class-level artifact pressure before deciding cleanup.

improvement_signal_review.symptom: Artifact footprint pressure required manual discovery across planning archives, reviews, memory, generated outputs, local-only state, and docs.

drift_log: 2026-04-28: Promoted after #450 closeout as the next concrete footprint/compression slice.
2026-04-28: Kept the answer selector-driven rather than adding another visible command or startup surface.

proof_report.validation proof: pytest workspace CLI, ruff, planning surface check, operational_compression report, proof selection, root CLI authority, planning doctor, start, and default report all passed.
proof_report.proof achieved now: yes; validation evidence is recorded in execution summary.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: pytest workspace CLI, ruff, planning surface check, operational_compression report, proof selection, root CLI authority, planning doctor, start, and default report all passed.
